articles.title: WITHOUT CODE
articles.summary: Anything lets you ship mobile apps, websites, or both — without writing a single line of code. Say goodbye to AI-looking templates: designs feel human, polished, and ready for launch [4]. Everything you need is built in, so you can go from idea to live product without hunting for extra tools.

articles.title: THE STATE OF AI 2025
articles.summary: AI startups are growing like nothing we've ever seen - some hit $100M ARR in their first year. Bessemer studied 20 companies and found two types: Supernovas that explode to $125M by year two but have 25% gross margins and fragile retention, and Shooting Stars that quadruple yearly with 60% margins. The new benchmark isn't T2D3 anymore, it's Q2T3 (quadruple, quadruple, triple, triple, triple).

articles.title: COHERE RAISES $500M AT $6.8B VALUATION
articles.summary: Cohere secured $500 million in an oversubscribed round led by Radical Ventures and Inovia Capital, propelled to a $6.8 billion valuation. The company added Joelle Pineau (former Meta AI research lead) as Chief AI Officer and Francois Chadwick (ex-Uber) as CFO. Cohere will invest in agentic AI tools like North, designed to automate enterprise workflows, while maintaining a security-first, on-premises deployment approach.

articles.title: JACK DORSEY: I THINK OF THE CEO ROLE AS "CHIEF EDITOR" OF THE COMPANY
articles.summary: Jack Dorsey runs Square like a magazine editor - thousands of ideas flood in from engineers, support, and designers, but only one or two really push the company forward. The CEO's job is to review everything and cut ruthlessly.

articles.title: YOUR PLAYBOOK TO FORECASTING PIPELINE AT SCALE
articles.summary: Rubrik hit $1 billion ARR with a forecasting process that starts five days before executives see numbers. Wednesday: reps validate every deal with managers. Monday: the CRO digs into each opportunity. Friday: C-suite gets a forecast that's been pressure-tested multiple times. It marks deals as "in-path" (leadership believes it) or "out-of-path" (risky). When a rep commits a deal but their manager disagrees, that's an automatic investigation. Quotas come from actual data - Rubrik calculates exactly how many calls turn into meetings or deals, so reps believe their targets are achievable. The company forces sales, marketing, and finance into weekly accountability forums where nobody can dodge revenue questions.

articles.title: TIME TO EXIT: ARE STARTUPS GETTING OLDER?
articles.summary: Startups are taking longer to exit, with the median age at IPO now at 13.5 years due to increased private capital availability, market pressures, and a focus on profitability. This trend results in longer holding periods for investors, a shift towards later-stage investing, and more emphasis on secondary sales for liquidity. Investors may adapt by extending fund lifecycles, diversifying investment stages, and prioritizing sustainable growth.

articles.title: ALIGNING PRODUCT AND GO-TO-MARKET WITH METRICS
articles.summary: Dan Teodosiu and a fellow Balderton Capital EIR discuss aligning product and go-to-market teams using customer-value metrics. The article outlines historical examples where companies faltered by neglecting customer value. It encourages leaders to focus on why customers would repeatedly choose their product.

articles.title: KNOWLIFY
articles.summary: Transform any content into explainer videos.

articles.title: DOLPHIN
articles.summary: Turn your customer calls into short, ready-to-share highlight reels.

articles.title: WHAT DO YOU MEAN?
articles.summary: We all use the same words, but mean completely different things. Your engineer says the feature is "done" (it's not deployed). Your designer says it's "done" (first draft). Your co-founder calls the new strategy "risky" (exciting!). You hear "risky" (we're gonna die). This stuff kills startups. One person's "by end of day" is 5 pm, another's is midnight. One founder's "love" for the company means 60-hour weeks, the other's means sustainable passion. The fix is stupidly simple: just define your terms. What does "shipped" mean at your company? What's "good enough"?

articles.title: WHEN BUILDING GETS EASY, WINNING GETS HARD
articles.summary: Sam Altman says we're entering the "fast fashion era of SaaS," and he's right. AI split software into two camps - the hard builds like infrastructure and foundation models, and the easy builds you can ship in a weekend with Cursor. When building gets easy, winning gets exponentially harder because everyone has the same product. The margin reality is brutal. Replit swings between -14% and +36% gross margins, while OpenAI sits at 45% and Nvidia at 75%.

articles.title: THE RED QUEEN EFFECT - MENTAL MODEL: WORK SMARTER, NOT FASTER
articles.summary: The Red Queen told Alice she must run twice as fast just to get anywhere because in Wonderland, running at full speed only keeps you in the same place. This perfectly describes startups that chase every AI update and framework just to avoid falling behind. Teams exhaust themselves implementing every new tool while competitors do the same, so nobody actually pulls ahead. We're so busy keeping up that we forget to ask if we're running in the right direction. Three questions cut through the panic: Are you making real progress or just matching competitors? Why are you moving in this direction? What would actually happen if you paused?

articles.title: PROFOUND SECURES $35M TO CONNECT BRANDS WITH ONE NEW CUSTOMER: SUPERINTELLIGENCE
articles.summary: Profound secured $35M in Series B funding led by Sequoia Capital to develop a platform that connects brands with AI (superintelligence) as a new customer base.

articles.title: GOOGLE VET RAISES $8M FOR CONTINUA TO BRING AI AGENTS TO GROUP CHATS
articles.summary: Continua, founded by ex-Google engineer David Petrou, raised $8 million to integrate AI agents in group chats on platforms like SMS and Discord, enhancing collaboration with automated features like reminders and polls.

articles.title: INDIA'S FIRST AI UNICORN FRACTAL FILES FOR $560 MILLION IPO: A STRATEGIC DEEP DIVE INTO AI'S EMERGING MARKET POTENTIAL
articles.summary: Fractal Analytics, India's first AI unicorn, is preparing for a $400-500 million IPO that will value the company at $3 billion, highlighting AI's growing importance in emerging markets.
